questions.question: What is the third month of Australia's financial year?
In the nursery rhyme, Three Blind Mice, who cut off the tails of the mice?
The two main types of commercial grain crops are legumes and ... what?
Which fruit has been banned on Singapore's public transport system since 1988?
In which videogame can players earn and spend "V-Bucks"?
Which late Bundjalung woman and broadcaster is credited with coining the term "Welcome to Country"?
What was the name of the capital of Roman Britain, founded around 47–50 AD?
Named for the German mineralogist Friedrich Mohs, what does the Mohs scale measure?
The TV series Bullpitt! was a spinoff from which other Australian sitcom?
"Sandgroper" is slang for a person from which state or territory?
What does Forrest call his shrimping business in the 1994 film Forrest Gump?
What word can be used for a ball game, a vine fruit and a verb meaning to crush?
The 1973 Oldsmobile Toronado was the first production car to be fitted with which safety device?
Which best-selling Haruki Murakami novel begins with the search for a lost cat?
True or false: our eardrums move in sync with our eyeballs?
Which Swedish pop singer is often described as the "Queen of Sad Bangers"?
The Empire State Building is an example of which architectural style?
Name three spices in classic Chinese five-spice powder.
questions.answer: Star anise, cloves, cinnamon, Sichuan pepper, fennel seeds

questions.question: Days We Left Behind is a recent single by which veteran rock musician?
In which Middle Eastern country is Jeddah the second-largest city?
Who was Barbra Streisand's first husband: ice-cream heir Richard Baskin, actor Elliott Gould or Canadian PM Pierre Trudeau?
Psoriasis primarily affects which bodily organ?
A high-protein meal trend among Gen-Z gym-goers is called "(what?) kibble".
Was the exclamation point first called the point of admiration, recognition or wonderment?
In the oil industry, a "barrel" is approximately equal to how many litres: 49, 119 or 159?
